title: Exit Strategy

jokes.text: Unbutton my shirt a bit and give him this look.
jokes.setup: Michael asks Lindsay how she'll respond to questions about financial improprieties at her deposition
jokes.punchline: Lindsay thinks the solution is to seduce the questioner rather than answer honestly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Lindsay's vapid approach to serious legal matters. Economy is excellent, character-specific.

jokes.text: Not really sure what you're going for there.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's inappropriate seduction strategy
jokes.punchline: Michael's understated confusion at her bizarre approach
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael straight-man response. Perfect understatement.

jokes.text: If I look like a man who made love to his wife last night, it's because I almost did.
jokes.setup: Tobias appears disheveled, setting up expectation he had a romantic evening
jokes.punchline: The reveal that he only 'almost' had sex deflates the boast entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. Sets up masculine bravado then deflates it perfectly. Pure Tobias - trying to sound impressive while revealing failure.

jokes.text: Well, I had to. It's vodka. It goes bad once it's opened.
jokes.setup: Question about why he finished the bottle
jokes.punchline: Completely false 'fact' about vodka delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias false confidence about something he knows nothing about. Sets up the callback to Lucille's fibs.

jokes.text: I think that's another one of Mom's little fibs. You know, like 'I'll sacrifice anything for my children.'
jokes.setup: Tobias's false fact about vodka
jokes.punchline: Lindsay connects it to their mother's lies, with a cutting example
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure that reveals family dynamics. Lindsay's casual delivery of a harsh truth about her mother.

jokes.text: I got a call out of the blue for a chance to play a very important part in The Prosecution.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining why he can't be deposed
jokes.punchline: He thinks 'the prosecution' is a TV show he's been cast in
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy. Tobias's delusion meets the reality of their legal troubles. The capitalization of 'The Prosecution' sells his misunderstanding.

jokes.text: I assume it's a CBS procedural.
jokes.setup: Continuing his delusion about being cast in 'The Prosecution'
jokes.punchline: Specific network assumption makes his delusion more elaborate
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the delusion with specific network reference that feels authentically Tobias.

jokes.text: Although they didn't send sides, so I thought I'd trot out a Vagina Monologue, or something else I know.
jokes.setup: Tobias preparing for his 'audition'
jokes.punchline: He plans to perform a Vagina Monologue, completely inappropriate for a male actor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - oblivious to gender appropriateness. 'Or something else I know' suggests limited repertoire.

jokes.text: For set dressing, silly.
jokes.setup: Michael asks why prosecution would want documents
jokes.punchline: Tobias thinks they need props for the TV show
jokes.explanation: Great continuation of the delusion. 'Silly' dismisses Michael's concern while revealing Tobias's complete misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Tell me that's not a CBS franchise.
jokes.setup: Michael explains the prosecution will bend the law to enforce the law
jokes.punchline: Tobias sees this as evidence it's a TV procedural format
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Tobias finds more 'evidence' for his delusion in Michael's serious warning.

jokes.text: Including me? Hmm? No. You don't have any secrets.
jokes.setup: George Michael asks if prosecution will come after him
jokes.punchline: Michael's dismissive response when George Michael clearly does have secrets
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - audience knows George Michael has huge secrets (Maeby relationship). Michael's casual dismissal is unintentionally wrong.

jokes.text: I was changing the subject. We-- no, yeah, we weren't talking about Maeby.
jokes.setup: George Michael awkwardly tries to change subject to Maeby's birthday
jokes.punchline: His flustered explanation draws more attention to what he's hiding
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - George Michael's awkward attempt to deflect makes things worse. The stammering sells his nervousness.

jokes.text: Actually, he'd already told her about it.
jokes.setup: George Michael talks about surprising Maeby
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals the surprise is already ruined
jokes.explanation: Classic narrator contradiction. Simple but effective undermining of character's plans.

jokes.text: Would it kill her to let some vodka go bad?
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining about helping drunk mother
jokes.punchline: References the earlier vodka myth, showing it's family-wide ignorance
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows the family's shared ignorance about alcohol. Lindsay adopts Tobias's false fact.

jokes.text: Family forgets Maeby's age - she says 16th birthday, Tobias thinks 14th
jokes.setup: Discussion about Maeby's upcoming birthday
jokes.punchline: Her own father doesn't know how old she is
jokes.explanation: Reinforces the theme of family neglect and Tobias's disconnection from his daughter

jokes.text: Oh, no, vodka doesn't... You know, it's my birthday in a week and I bet you they don't even know.
jokes.setup: Maeby starts to correct the vodka myth
jokes.punchline: She gives up and pivots to complaining about being ignored
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Maeby's resignation to family dysfunction. She knows better but can't be bothered to correct them.

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh. Our little girl's turning 14.
jokes.setup: Discussing Maeby's 16th birthday
jokes.punchline: Tobias gets her age wrong by two years
jokes.explanation: Classic inattentive father joke. Simple but effective at showing how disconnected Tobias is from his daughter.

jokes.text: That is her suit. They probably don't make it in a woman's though. They only make it in a woman's.
jokes.setup: Tobias suggests buying Maeby a suit like the one he's wearing
jokes.punchline: Lindsay reveals it IS Maeby's suit and women's clothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal that Tobias is wearing women's clothing without realizing it. Perfect character comedy that builds the gender-confusion running gag.

jokes.text: You see, sometimes shame can be fun. Shame? Secrets. You said 'shame.'
jokes.setup: Tobias tries to say 'secrets' but says 'shame'
jokes.punchline: Lindsay catches his Freudian slip
jokes.explanation: Freudian slip reveals Tobias's subconscious feelings. Lindsay's correction highlights the error.

jokes.text: Gob plans to go to Iraq for a 'USO tour' doing Christian magic
jokes.setup: Gob announces his travel plans
jokes.punchline: He's combining military entertainment with religious magic tricks
jokes.explanation: Absurd combination that perfectly fits Gob's misguided ambition and poor judgment

jokes.text: How does he know about her? Didn't he say that Gob should be ashamed of himself? No, but give it a little while, somebody will.
jokes.setup: Confusion about what Tobias meant by 'shame'
jokes.punchline: Casual prediction that Gob will do something shameful soon
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about Gob's predictable behavior patterns. The certainty of the prediction is the joke.

jokes.text: It's 'water into wine.' Then I emptied out all your wine bottles and filled them with fake stuff for nothing.
jokes.setup: Gob explains his Christian magic act
jokes.punchline: He got the miracle backwards and wasted Lindsay's real wine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - incompetent at basic knowledge while being confident. The waste of real wine for fake wine adds to the stupidity.

jokes.text: Great, how am I supposed to have sex with my husband now?
jokes.setup: Learning that her wine has been replaced with fake stuff
jokes.punchline: Lindsay needs alcohol to have sex with Tobias
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest admission about their marriage. Lindsay's casual delivery of something so harsh makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'A flower in my garden, a mystery in my panties.'
jokes.setup: Tobias at what he thinks is a TV audition
jokes.punchline: He performs inappropriate sexual dialogue from Vagina Monologues
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Tobias performing female-specific sexual content for prosecutors. The contrast between his earnestness and inappropriateness is perfect.

jokes.text: Again, this is not an audition for a CBS show.
jokes.setup: Tobias performing Vagina Monologue
jokes.punchline: Prosecutor's deadpan clarification after bizarre performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. 'Again' suggests this has been explained before, adding to the absurdity.

jokes.text: He took the bait. He's a classic scrapbooker. Right down to the woman's suit.
jokes.setup: Tobias sees flyer for scrapbooking
jokes.punchline: Prosecutors profiling him, noting his women's clothing
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the suit joke. The idea that wearing women's clothing makes him a 'classic scrapbooker' is absurd profiling.

jokes.text: I've got to find a loophole to get out of this, but Buster took coma, and your mother took rehab.
jokes.setup: George Sr. explaining his legal situation
jokes.punchline: He describes fake medical conditions as if they're strategic resources to be allocated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - treats serious medical conditions like strategy game resources. The casual 'took' implies they're claiming them like items.

jokes.text: Well, wine only turns into alcohol if you let it sit.
jokes.setup: Question about wine at rehab
jokes.punchline: Another completely false 'fact' about alcohol delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the vodka myth. Shows the family's systematic ignorance about alcohol comes from Lucille.

jokes.text: That's okay. Try again... No, that's a tendon. He'd be screaming right now. It's okay. try again.
jokes.setup: Medical students practicing on comatose Buster
jokes.punchline: They're clearly hurting him but instructor encourages them to keep trying
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy. The instructor's encouraging tone contrasts with the obvious pain being inflicted.

jokes.text: That's okay, you put too much collagen in there. No, no, try it again.
jokes.setup: Buster being used by beauty students
jokes.punchline: They're botching cosmetic procedures on him
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalating abuse of fake-coma Buster. The cheerful instructor tone adds to the dark humor.

jokes.text: Oops, you've got your forearm right on his windpipe there, Dogbone.
jokes.setup: At-risk youth dental program
jokes.punchline: Student is accidentally choking Buster while working on his teeth
jokes.explanation: Peak of the escalating abuse sequence. The casual 'oops' and name 'Dogbone' add character to the scene.

jokes.text: It really was amazing for their self-esteem. I did it.
jokes.setup: Students practicing on fake coma patient
jokes.punchline: Their 'success' is just Buster being helpful
jokes.explanation: Great irony. Shows the unintended consequences of Buster's deception benefiting others.

jokes.text: Faker! She was wrong on that one. It was love that made Gary take that step.
jokes.setup: Adelaide calling everyone fakers
jokes.punchline: She calls actually disabled Gary a faker when he tries to walk for love
jokes.explanation: Dark irony - the one person she calls faker is actually genuine. Narrator's clarification adds to the tragic comedy.

jokes.text: Of course, he hadn't used his legs in three weeks.
jokes.setup: Gary attempts to walk to show love
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals the medical reality of his situation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical realities. Good structure with delayed consequences.

jokes.text: But we do know that the two people who checked into Shady Pines under the names Lucille Bluth and Lindsay Funke are not your relatives.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming his family is in legitimate rehab
jokes.punchline: Prosecutor reveals they sent imposters to rehab
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that exposes the family's elaborate deception. The specificity of having replacement people makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'She's Out Of My Life.' As for your brother Gob in the USO... Don't tell me he's not in Iraq. Oh, no, he's there.
jokes.setup: Michael expecting to hear Gob lied about going to Iraq
jokes.punchline: Gob actually did go but got arrested
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - sets up expectation that Gob is lying, then reveals he's telling the truth but in trouble anyway.

jokes.text: Cool your Japanese jets.
jokes.setup: Someone wanting to go online immediately
jokes.punchline: Malapropism mixing 'cool your jets' with 'Japanese' reference
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke. Not particularly strong but has quotable quality due to the absurd phrase.

jokes.text: And imagine the impact if that had come on right when we turned on the TV.
jokes.setup: Trying to find news about Gob
jokes.punchline: Narrator comments on the failed comedic timing
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about TV comedy conventions. Self-aware about missing the perfect moment.

jokes.text: I'm not faking it, Michael. I really am this expressionless.
jokes.setup: Buster told to stop pretending to be in coma
jokes.punchline: He claims his natural expression is so blank it looks comatose
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy that's also meta - Buster is indeed naturally expressionless. Self-aware humor about his personality.

jokes.text: No, I was just clearing my throat, but I think it actually does mean 'laundry' but like, a child's laundry. We don't really have a word for it.
jokes.setup: Asked if he speaks Arabic
jokes.punchline: Throat clearing might coincidentally mean very specific type of laundry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy. The specificity of 'child's laundry' and claim we need a word for it is perfectly bizarre Buster logic.

jokes.text: Buster claims he speaks Arabic but just clears his throat: 'La-ach-ha-tem'
jokes.setup: Michael asks if Buster speaks Arabic
jokes.punchline: Buster's 'Arabic' is just throat clearing that he thinks means 'child's laundry'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment combining false confidence with complete incompetence

jokes.text: 'My Favorite Birthday,' 'Foreign Bank Statements,' and of course, 'Shh! Family Secrets.'
jokes.setup: Scrapbooking class supplies templates
jokes.punchline: Innocent craft activity designed to gather evidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept. The juxtaposition of innocent crafting with federal investigation is perfect.

jokes.text: ...Oliver North but ironically not Martha Stewart.
jokes.setup: Listing people caught by scrapbooking sting
jokes.punchline: Martha Stewart, the craft expert, wasn't caught by a crafting scheme
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - the person most associated with crafts avoided the craft-based sting. Great economy and clever observation.

jokes.text: Wow, this is the best free scrapbooking class I've ever taken.
jokes.setup: Tobias being offered helicopter to get evidence
jokes.punchline: He still thinks this is a genuine craft class despite obvious investigation
jokes.explanation: Great character blindness - even a helicopter doesn't tip him off. 'Ever taken' implies he's a scrapbooking regular.

jokes.text: What the hell are we supposed to do for two days. I could take the top off this grape juice and maybe by morning...
jokes.setup: Lucille at spa with no alcohol
jokes.punchline: She thinks grape juice can ferment into wine overnight
jokes.explanation: Another alcohol myth from Lucille. Shows her desperation and continued ignorance about fermentation timelines.

jokes.text: Oh, Buster, you're such a ( bleep ). That means she likes you. Oh. It's more than like.
jokes.setup: Adelaide appears to insult Buster with bleeped word
jokes.punchline: The censored word is actually a term of endearment
jokes.explanation: Clever use of censorship expectations. Audience assumes it's profanity but it's revealed as affection from foreign character.

jokes.text: When I miss your lips, I'll put a fig in my mouth and think of you.
jokes.setup: Buster making romantic declaration before leaving
jokes.punchline: Bizarre analogy using figs as lip substitute
jokes.explanation: Peak Buster romantic awkwardness. The fig analogy is so specific and weird it's perfect for his character. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I don't think Steve Holt has to come to this. And yet he did invite Mickey Rourke.
jokes.setup: George Michael screening Maeby's party guest list
jokes.punchline: He excludes teenage rival but invites random celebrity
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd logic - excludes peer out of jealousy but includes inappropriate celebrity. The randomness of Mickey Rourke makes it funnier.

jokes.text: So you ready for Operation Hot Brother?
jokes.setup: Planning rescue mission
jokes.punchline: Buster's inappropriate military operation name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Shows Buster's military wannabe tendencies and poor naming sense.

jokes.text: The King of Queens. And now I would like to behold one of the late great Jesus' biggest illusions, The Burning Bush.
jokes.setup: Gob performing Christian magic referring to 'King of Kings'
jokes.punchline: He says 'King of Queens' and calls Jesus 'the late great'
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Gob's ignorance of religion despite attempting religious magic. The TV show reference and 'late great' phrasing reveal his mindset.

jokes.text: Burn Bush, burn Bush, burn Bush, burn Bush...
jokes.setup: Gob trying to do burning bush miracle
jokes.punchline: Crowd interprets it as anti-Bush political chant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political comedy that works on multiple levels. Gob's innocent magic creates anti-American demonstration. Perfect timing with Iraq War context.

jokes.text: I'm sorry it took so long, but the Cheney Expressway was backed up all the way to Halliburton Drive.
jokes.setup: Apology for traffic delay
jokes.punchline: Iraqi roads named after American war profiteers
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire embedded in mundane traffic complaint. The absurd idea that Iraq would name roads after American officials is brilliant.

jokes.text: Saddam look-alike: 'Take my picture! Take my picture!'
jokes.setup: Michael discovers someone who looks like Saddam
jokes.punchline: The look-alike is excited about photo opportunities
jokes.explanation: Establishes the surreal situation of celebrity impersonators in Iraq

jokes.text: Yes, and we wanted to keep the house for just one more season.
jokes.setup: Saddam look-alikes discussing losing their jobs
jokes.punchline: TV show language about keeping the house 'one more season'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the show's own renewal struggles. Look-alikes use TV terminology that mirrors the show's situation.

jokes.text: Surprise. Yeah, that's what I figured.
jokes.setup: George Michael at failed surprise party
jokes.punchline: Maeby's unsurprised reaction to having no friends show up
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Maeby's resignation to social rejection is both sad and funny. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: They found out I was 16. And a lot of us just didn't want to drive down to Orange County.
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining why friends didn't come
jokes.punchline: Age revelation plus geographic inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the age deception storyline. The casual addition of geographic complaints is realistic and funny.

jokes.text: Maeby and George Michael drink Gob's fake wine, thinking it's alcohol
jokes.setup: They decide to drink together
jokes.punchline: The wine is actually non-alcoholic from Gob's magic supplies
jokes.explanation: Ironic setup for their romantic encounter using fake alcohol

jokes.text: My God, it makes me want to have an ice-cream cone. Doesn't this make you want to have an ice-cream cone?
jokes.setup: Buster examining nuclear warhead
jokes.punchline: WMD inspection triggers ice cream craving
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Buster moment. The complete disconnect between deadly weapons and ice cream is brilliantly childlike.

jokes.text: Look, it's a Homefill. It's the company that makes the fake stuff we fill the model homes with.
jokes.setup: Discovery of what appears to be WMD
jokes.punchline: It's just fake furniture stuffing from model homes
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of tension. The serious WMD hunt resolves to mundane furniture stuffing, perfect for the show's tone.

jokes.text: 'Cause Gangee once said to me, your mom might not have ever even been pregnant with you.
jokes.setup: Buster trying to comfort Maeby about not being part of family
jokes.punchline: He suggests she might not be biologically related
jokes.explanation: Dark humor disguised as comfort. Buster's well-meaning attempt to help actually suggests something more disturbing about Maeby's origins.

jokes.text: And perhaps it was the fake wine, but that was the night George Michael and Maeby went to second base.
jokes.setup: George Michael and Maeby drinking what they think is alcohol
jokes.punchline: Fake wine leads to cousin romance progression
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy payoff that connects multiple storylines. The fake wine callback enables the taboo relationship progression.

jokes.text: We call them 'unintentional operations victims,' but yes.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if his father was a patsy
jokes.punchline: CIA euphemism for 'patsy' is absurdly bureaucratic
jokes.explanation: Good satire of government euphemisms. The overly clinical term for 'patsy' reflects bureaucratic language avoidance.

jokes.text: I think he means to shoot you in the head. But please, at least let him look at the air-conditioning.
jokes.setup: Confusion about being 'taken out'
jokes.punchline: Michael calmly clarifies they'll be murdered but still prioritizes the repair job
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - deadpan reaction to death threat while maintaining professional obligations. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I think he means to shoot you in the head.
jokes.setup: Confusion about 'taking them out'
jokes.punchline: Buster matter-of-factly explains assassination threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster deadpan delivery. His casual explanation of mortal danger is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Buster: 'Looks like you are a hero, Buster.' 'We both are.' 'Well...'
jokes.setup: Michael calls Buster a hero
jokes.punchline: Michael's hesitation when Buster tries to share the credit
jokes.explanation: Subtle character beat showing Michael's ego even in victory

jokes.text: And direct flights. None of this stopping in Phoenix bull ( bleep ).
jokes.setup: Negotiating demands with CIA
jokes.punchline: Gob's biggest concern is flight connections
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob priorities - even when negotiating with CIA, his main concern is travel convenience. Very character-specific complaint.

jokes.text: Take Condoleezza Rice Lane. It's quicker. Mission accomplished.
jokes.setup: Getting directions in Iraq
jokes.punchline: More absurd American-named streets plus Bush's premature victory declaration
jokes.explanation: Good callback to street naming joke with added reference to Bush's infamous aircraft carrier banner. Multiple political layers.

tmdb_overview: Gob goes to Iraq to perform his Christian magic act on the USO tour and ends up incarcerated. Michael learns George, Sr. sent Gob to Iraq to burn down the model home he built there, so Michael and Buster fly to Iraq to spring their brother from prison.
insight: Season 3's tightest finale: 67 jokes in 28 minutes, balancing chaos with character irony.
